title: GitHub Copilot Features
description: A comprehensive overview of GitHub Copilot plans, subscription tiers, and AI-powered features for developers and teams.
intro: This page provides a comprehensive overview of GitHub Copilot plans, combining official features with example videos.
note: GitHub Copilot features like the Coding Agent are not currently available in GitHub Enterprise Server.

subscriptionTiers.name: Free
subscriptionTiers.tagline: A fast way to get started with GitHub Copilot
features: Access to Claude Haiku 4.5, GPT-4.1, GPT-5 mini, and more

subscriptionTiers.name: Student
features: Unlimited agent mode and chats with GPT-5 mini
Access to code review, Claude Sonnet 4/4.5, GPT-5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and more

subscriptionTiers.name: Pro
subscriptionTiers.tagline: Accelerate workflows with GitHub Copilot ($10 USD per month or $100 per year)
features: Unlimited agent mode and chats with GPT-5 mini
Access to code review, Claude Sonnet 4/4.5, GPT-5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and more
300 premium requests to use latest models, with option to buy more
Free for verified teachers and maintainers of popular open source projects

subscriptionTiers.name: Business
subscriptionTiers.tagline: For teams and organizations ($19 USD per user per month)
features: Data excluded from training by default
300 premium requests per user per month

subscriptionTiers.name: Pro+
subscriptionTiers.tagline: Scale with agents and more models ($39 USD per month or $390 per year)
features: Access to all models, including Claude Opus 4.5/4.6 and more
1,500 premium requests per month, with option to buy more

subscriptionTiers.name: Enterprise
subscriptionTiers.tagline: Full enterprise features and controls ($39 USD per user per month)
features: 1,000 premium requests per user per month

timelineFeatures.title: AI Code Completions
timelineFeatures.description: Real-time AI-powered inline code suggestions as you type, supporting dozens of languages and frameworks directly in your editor.

timelineFeatures.title: GitHub Copilot for Business
timelineFeatures.description: Enterprise-grade GitHub Copilot with organization policy management, user management, and data privacy controls for teams.

timelineFeatures.title: Copilot Chat
timelineFeatures.description: Conversational AI assistant embedded in your IDE to explain code, answer programming questions, and generate solutions with natural language.

timelineFeatures.title: Pull Request Summaries
timelineFeatures.description: Automatically generate clear, contextual summaries for pull requests, reducing review time and improving team communication.

timelineFeatures.title: GitHub Copilot Enterprise
timelineFeatures.description: Full enterprise capabilities including Copilot knowledge bases built from your own codebase, advanced security policies, and audit logs.

timelineFeatures.title: Copilot in the CLI
timelineFeatures.description: Get shell command suggestions and explanations from Copilot directly in your terminal, helping you discover and use CLI tools more effectively.

timelineFeatures.title: Copilot Extensions
timelineFeatures.description: Extend GitHub Copilot Chat with third-party integrations, letting developers interact with external tools and services without leaving their IDE.

timelineFeatures.title: Copilot in GitHub Mobile
timelineFeatures.description: Access Copilot Chat on the go via GitHub Mobile to review pull requests, understand code changes, and get answers about your repositories.

timelineFeatures.title: Copilot Knowledge Bases
timelineFeatures.description: Build custom knowledge bases from your organisation's code and documentation so Copilot Chat answers questions grounded in your own context.

timelineFeatures.title: GitHub Copilot Free Tier
timelineFeatures.description: Free access to GitHub Copilot for all individual developers, with 2,000 code completions and 50 premium chat requests per month at no cost.

timelineFeatures.title: Copilot Code Review
timelineFeatures.description: AI-powered pull request reviews that detect bugs, explain code changes, and suggest improvements, helping teams ship higher-quality code faster.

timelineFeatures.title: Copilot Coding Agent
timelineFeatures.description: An autonomous AI coding agent that can take on entire GitHub Issues, write code, run tests, and open pull requests with minimal human input.

timelineFeatures.title: Agent Mode in VS Code
timelineFeatures.description: Multi-step agentic AI workflows in Visual Studio Code, enabling Copilot to plan, edit multiple files, run terminal commands, and iterate autonomously.

timelineFeatures.title: Claude & Multi-Model Support
timelineFeatures.description: Choose from a growing roster of frontier AI models — including Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, and OpenAI GPT models — powering Copilot across IDEs.

timelineFeatures.title: GitHub Spark
timelineFeatures.description: A natural-language-first micro app builder powered by Copilot, allowing anyone to create and deploy personal web apps from a simple text description.

timelineFeatures.title: Third-Party Coding Agents
timelineFeatures.description: Connect external AI coding agents (such as Devin, Cursor, and others) to GitHub issues and pull requests via the Copilot Extensions platform.
